title: The Robin Williams Factor
wikitext:
[]: The Robin Williams Factor             Quote Post by Corb » Wed Jul 21, 2010 8:44 pm So, we all know that Robin Williams is an outspoken NGE fan... I'm flipping through this ancient (I think '98) issue of Animerica with a Sadamoto interview, and I see... Small world?

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Wed Jul 21, 2010 9:03 pm I like Robin Williams, and it's awesome to know hes a NGE fan. Sadamoto recognizing him doubles his already high status. In my world anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Jul 21, 2010 9:19 pm Mutual "fan"ship. It's amazing.

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel » Wed Jul 21, 2010 9:21 pm Can you imagine how awesome it would be to find out someone like Sadamoto based a character on you? Provided that the character in question wasn't an ass, of course.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Wed Jul 21, 2010 9:40 pm Haven't seen "The Wings of Honneamise", but interesting find. ...wait, is Robin Williams really a "NGE" fan? For a comedian, I wouldn't see him as a NGE fan.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Wed Jul 21, 2010 10:25 pm Nice find. For those who are wondering, yes, Robin Williams is a big fan of Evangelion. You can spot a Mass-Production Eva in One Hour Photo , and IIRC he contributed to a demo reel where he dubbed Fuyutsuki. Ramiel wrote: Can you imagine how awesome it would be to find out someone like Sadamoto based a character on you? Provided that the character in question wasn't an ass, of course.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Jul 21, 2010 11:18 pm The entirety of that interview was transcribed and can be found at the EML archives: http://eva.onegeek.org/pipermail/oldeva/1999-January/024322.html

[]:             Quote Post by Bomby von Bombsville » Wed Jul 21, 2010 11:30 pm The Six Degrees of Robin Williams!! Unfortunately, Eva is somehow linked to Patch Adams in two. -_-'

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Thu Jul 22, 2010 12:06 am drinian wrote: Nice find. For those who are wondering, yes, Robin Williams is a big fan of Evangelion. You can spot a Mass-Production Eva in One Hour Photo , and IIRC he contributed to a demo reel where he dubbed Fuyutsuki. Gunbuster's Smith Toren is named after a real person.

[]:             Quote Post by duexmachina » Thu Jul 22, 2010 12:22 am Bomby von Bombsville wrote: The Six Degrees of Robin Williams!! Unfortunately, Eva is somehow linked to Patch Adams in two. -_-'

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Jul 22, 2010 1:22 am drinian wrote: Nice find. For those who are wondering, yes, Robin Williams is a big fan of Evangelion. You can spot a Mass-Production Eva in One Hour Photo , and IIRC he contributed to a demo reel where he dubbed Fuyutsuki.

[]: Arabian NIIIIIGHTS like Arabian DAAAAAYS             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Thu Jul 22, 2010 9:17 am Sachi wrote: Robin Williams was interested into doing the NME dub, but nothing ever came out of it.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Thu Jul 22, 2010 1:40 pm duexmachina wrote: really? What scene?

[]:             Quote Post by DevRei17 » Thu Jul 22, 2010 4:59 pm I know someone who can also link Robin Williams and Eva. Two words: Koichi Yamadera.

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Fri Jul 23, 2010 8:30 am The MP Eva in One Hour Photo clued me in Which reminds me that I'm actually going to be watching that film this weekend!

[]:             Quote Post by Corb » Fri Jul 23, 2010 4:58 pm One Hour Photo is, IMO, an amazing movie though - creepy Robin is awesome - and Mark Romanek is a great director. Robin's apparently an avid gamer too. I bet he would be awesome to hang out with.

[]:             Quote Post by KLVTZ » Fri Jul 23, 2010 5:30 pm Corb wrote: One Hour Photo is, IMO, an amazing movie though - creepy Robin is awesome - and Mark Romanek is a great director. Robin's apparently an avid gamer too. I bet he would be awesome to hang out with.

[]: Robin Williams can do no wrong. Except Old Dogs.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Fri Jul 23, 2010 6:36 pm KLVTZ wrote: I like seeing the Evangelion model in the movie, i just wish they would pronounce the name with a hard G not a soft G

[]: Re: Robin Williams can do no wrong. Except Old Dogs.             Quote Post by Halicat » Fri Jul 23, 2010 6:46 pm Killer Bee wrote: Surely Robin Williams must've pronounced it correctly?

[]: Re: Robin Williams can do no wrong. Except Old Dogs.             Quote Post by KLVTZ » Sat Jul 24, 2010 12:06 am Halicat wrote: No, oddly enough :( I just checked, in fact he says 'Evan Genesis EvanJelion' D:
